signals.reasoning: Cover the live SOL short immediately. The loss has narrowed, but the position is still underwater at about $26.60 after previously being up about $37.42, retained none of peak P&L, is still flagged urgent, has no stop, and liquidation distance is still under 1%, so this remains failed-position cleanup rather than a real hold.

BTC still shows trend-supported strength with both price and open interest higher, and the new veto headline keeps fresh action defensive. That makes BTC watch-only rather than a fresh long or short here.

ETH remains structurally firm after the squeeze and the Bitmine treasury headline reinforces the upside narrative more than the downside one. Under veto there is no reason to force a new trade into that backdrop.
TST is still in an active squeeze with extreme price and open-interest expansion, which makes it too extended to chase and too unstable to fade. It stays sidelined.
FARTCOIN still has constructive continuation structure, but it is already crowded and well off the early-entry point. It does not outrank the need to flatten SOL risk.
GRIFFAIN reads like a completed long-liquidation flush rather than a fresh short with easy follow-through. In a veto regime that is a pass, not a chase.
CC has squeeze-style support from negative funding, but the move is already underway and open-interest history is too incomplete to trust as a fresh long in a veto window.
MON is still trending higher with positive funding and rising open interest, so it does not offer a clean short and is already too extended to buy. Watch-only.
LDO is one of the stronger upside continuation names on the board, but the rebound is already well developed and the veto state keeps it from becoming an active long.
KNEIRO has momentum but the chart is already stretched and the open-interest support is incomplete. That keeps it in watch mode only.
XPL has rebound strength, but it is already extended and not well-supported enough to justify action under veto. It stays sidelined.
